question: What is the Rule of Modularity in Unix philosophy?
ground_truth_answer: Write simple parts connected by clean interfaces
context_hint: Rule of Modularity: Write simple parts connected by clean interfaces.

question: What does the Rule of Clarity state?
ground_truth_answer: Clarity is better than cleverness
context_hint: Rule of Clarity: Clarity is better than cleverness.

question: What does the Rule of Composition say about program design?
ground_truth_answer: Design programs to be connected with other programs
context_hint: Rule of Composition: Design programs to be connected with other programs.

question: What does the Rule of Separation state?
ground_truth_answer: Separate policy from mechanism; separate interfaces from engines
context_hint: Rule of Separation: Separate policy from mechanism;

question: What does the Rule of Simplicity say about complexity?
ground_truth_answer: Design for simplicity; add complexity only where you must
context_hint: Rule of Simplicity: Design for simplicity; add complexity only where you must.

question: What does the Rule of Parsimony say about writing big programs?
ground_truth_answer: Write a big program only when it is clear by demonstration that nothing else will do
context_hint: Rule of Parsimony: Write a big program only when it is

question: What does the Rule of Transparency advocate for?
ground_truth_answer: Design for visibility to make inspection and debugging easier
context_hint: Rule of Transparency: Design for visibility to make inspection and debugging easier.

question: What is the Rule of Robustness derived from according to Unix philosophy?
ground_truth_answer: Robustness is the child of transparency and simplicity
context_hint: Rule of Robustness: Robustness is the child of transparency and simplicity.

question: What does the Rule of Representation advocate?
ground_truth_answer: Fold knowledge into data so program logic can be stupid and robust
context_hint: Rule of Representation: Fold knowledge into data,

question: What does the Rule of Least Surprise say about interface design?
ground_truth_answer: Always do the least surprising thing
context_hint: Rule of Least Surprise: In interface design, always do the least surprising thing.

question: What does the Rule of Silence prescribe when a program has nothing surprising to say?
ground_truth_answer: It should say nothing
context_hint: Rule of Silence: When a program has nothing surprising to say, it should say nothing.

question: What does the Rule of Repair say about failure?
ground_truth_answer: Repair what you can, but when you must fail, fail noisily and as soon as possible
context_hint: Rule of Repair: Repair what you can

question: What does the Rule of Economy say about programmer time versus machine time?
ground_truth_answer: Programmer time is expensive; conserve it in preference to machine time
context_hint: Rule of Economy: Programmer time is expensive; conserve it in preference to machine time.

question: What does the Rule of Generation say about hand-hacking?
ground_truth_answer: Avoid hand-hacking; write programs to write programs when you can
context_hint: Rule of Generation: Avoid hand-hacking; write programs to write programs when you can.

question: What does the Rule of Diversity say about claiming one true way?
ground_truth_answer: Distrust all claims for one true way
context_hint: Rule of Diversity: Distrust all claims for one true way.

question: What does the Rule of Extensibility say about the future?
ground_truth_answer: Design for the future, because it will be here sooner than you think
context_hint: Rule of Extensibility: Design for the future, because it will be here sooner than you think.

question: What was the key innovation that Unix introduced for connecting programs?
ground_truth_answer: The pipeline for connecting programs
context_hint: the pipeline for connecting programs.

question: What is the Unix philosophy rule about making each program do?
ground_truth_answer: Make each program do one thing well
context_hint: (i) Make each program do one thing well.

question: What does the Rule of Optimization say about prototyping?
ground_truth_answer: Prototype before polishing; get it working before you optimize it
context_hint: Rule of Optimization: Prototype before polishing. Get it

question: What section covers the basics of Unix philosophy in the Art of Unix Programming?
ground_truth_answer: The section titled Basics of the Unix Philosophy
context_hint: Basics of the Unix Philosophy

question: What does Unix philosophy say about interface design and surprise?
ground_truth_answer: Always do the least surprising thing in interface design
context_hint: In interface design, always do the least surprising thing.

question: What qualities of Unix are highlighted as making it durable?
ground_truth_answer: Unix culture, open-source software, cross-platform portability and open standards, and the Internet
context_hint: The Durability of Unix

question: What does the Rule of Composition enable in Unix?
ground_truth_answer: Programs to be connected with other programs, enabling pipelines and composable tools
context_hint: Rule of Composition: Design programs to be connected to other programs.

question: What does the Unix philosophy say about program failure modes?
ground_truth_answer: When you must fail, fail noisily and as soon as possible
context_hint: Rule of Repair: When you must fail, fail noisily and

question: What does the Unix philosophy identify as a benefit of open-source software?
ground_truth_answer: Open-source software is listed as one of the things Unix gets right
context_hint: Open-Source Software

question: What does Rule of Transparency say about debugging?
ground_truth_answer: Design for visibility to make inspection and debugging easier
context_hint: Design for visibility to make inspection and debugging easier.

question: According to Unix philosophy, what should you do when writing a new job?
ground_truth_answer: Build a fresh tool; do not add a new job to an existing program
context_hint: To do a new job, build

question: What does the Rule of Representation say about data and program logic?
ground_truth_answer: Fold knowledge into data so program logic can be stupid and robust
context_hint: Fold knowledge into data so program logic can be stupid and robust.

question: What sections of Unix philosophy does the Art of Unix Programming cover?
ground_truth_answer: Culture, durability, what Unix gets wrong, what Unix gets right, open-source software, internet, basics of the Unix philosophy, and more

question: What does the Unix philosophy say about complexity?
ground_truth_answer: Design for simplicity and add complexity only where you must
